---
title: Media API
description: Manage images and videos from camera traps
---

## Overview

The Media API handles image and video files captured by camera traps. Media files are linked to deployments and can have multiple observations. All media endpoints are defined in `src/redux/api/media/index.ts`.

## Available Hooks

```typescript
import {
  useGetMediaQuery,
  useGetMediaByIdQuery,
  useCreateMediaMutation,
  useUpdateMediaMutation,
  useDeleteMediaMutation,
} from '@/redux/api/media'
```

**Note:** The hooks are not explicitly exported in the source file but are auto-generated by RTK Query.

## Media Types

```typescript
type Media = {
  id: string
  mediaID: string
  deploymentID?: string
  fileMediaType?: string
  filePath?: string
  filePublic?: boolean
  exifData?: Record<string, any>
  observations?: string[]
  sensorRecords?: string[]
}

type MediaCreate = Omit<Media, "id" | "mediaID">
type MediaUpdate = MediaCreate & {
  id: string
}
```

## Get All Media

Fetch a list of all media files.

### Hook

```typescript
useGetMediaQuery()
```

### Parameters

None

### Response

Returns an array of `Media` objects.

<ResponseField name="Media[]" type="array">
  Array of media file records
  
  <ResponseField name="id" type="string">
    Internal unique identifier
  </ResponseField>
  
  <ResponseField name="mediaID" type="string">
    Media file identifier
  </ResponseField>
  
  <ResponseField name="deploymentID" type="string" optional>
    Associated deployment ID
  </ResponseField>
  
  <ResponseField name="fileMediaType" type="string" optional>
    Media type (e.g., "image/jpeg", "video/mp4")
  </ResponseField>
  
  <ResponseField name="filePath" type="string" optional>
    URL or path to the media file
  </ResponseField>
  
  <ResponseField name="filePublic" type="boolean" optional>
    Whether the file is publicly accessible
  </ResponseField>
  
  <ResponseField name="exifData" type="object" optional>
    EXIF metadata extracted from the file
  </ResponseField>
  
  <ResponseField name="observations" type="string[]" optional>
    Array of observation IDs linked to this media
  </ResponseField>
  
  <ResponseField name="sensorRecords" type="string[]" optional>
    Array of sensor record IDs linked to this media
  </ResponseField>
</ResponseField>

### Usage Example

<CodeGroup>

```typescript Component
import { useGetMediaQuery } from '@/redux/api/media'
import { Image } from 'react-native'

function MediaGallery() {
  const { data: mediaFiles, isLoading } = useGetMediaQuery()

  if (isLoading) return <Loading />

  const images = mediaFiles?.filter(m => m.fileMediaType?.startsWith('image/'))
  const videos = mediaFiles?.filter(m => m.fileMediaType?.startsWith('video/'))

  return (
    <div>
      <h2>Images ({images?.length})</h2>
      <div className="grid">
        {images?.map(media => (
          <div key={media.id}>
            <Image source={{ uri: media.filePath }} />
            <p>{media.observations?.length || 0} observations</p>
          </div>
        ))}
      </div>
      
      <h2>Videos ({videos?.length})</h2>
      <div className="grid">
        {videos?.map(media => (
          <div key={media.id}>
            <video src={media.filePath} controls />
          </div>
        ))}
      </div>
    </div>
  )
}
```

```typescript Response
[
  {
    "id": "media_789",
    "mediaID": "IMG-20240315-142300",
    "deploymentID": "dep_456",
    "fileMediaType": "image/jpeg",
    "filePath": "https://storage.example.com/media/IMG-20240315-142300.jpg",
    "filePublic": false,
    "exifData": {
      "DateTimeOriginal": "2024-03-15T14:23:00",
      "Make": "Bushnell",
      "Model": "Trophy Cam",
      "GPSLatitude": "-3.4653",
      "GPSLongitude": "-62.2159"
    },
    "observations": ["obs_001"],
    "sensorRecords": ["sensor_001"]
  }
]
```

</CodeGroup>

## Get Media by ID

Fetch a single media file by its ID.

### Hook

```typescript
useGetMediaByIdQuery(id: string)
```

### Parameters

<ParamField path="id" type="string" required>
  The unique media ID
</ParamField>

### Response

Returns a single `Media` object.

### Usage Example

<CodeGroup>

```typescript Component
import { useGetMediaByIdQuery } from '@/redux/api/media'
import { Image } from 'react-native'

function MediaViewer({ mediaId }: { mediaId: string }) {
  const { data: media, isLoading } = useGetMediaByIdQuery(mediaId)

  if (isLoading) return <Loading />
  if (!media) return <NotFound />

  const isImage = media.fileMediaType?.startsWith('image/')
  const isVideo = media.fileMediaType?.startsWith('video/')

  return (
    <div>
      <h2>{media.mediaID}</h2>
      
      {isImage && <Image source={{ uri: media.filePath }} style={{ width: '100%' }} />}
      {isVideo && <video src={media.filePath} controls style={{ width: '100%' }} />}
      
      {media.exifData && (
        <div>
          <h3>Metadata</h3>
          <pre>{JSON.stringify(media.exifData, null, 2)}</pre>
        </div>
      )}
      
      <p>Observations: {media.observations?.length || 0}</p>
      <p>Public: {media.filePublic ? 'Yes' : 'No'}</p>
    </div>
  )
}
```

```typescript Response
{
  "id": "media_789",
  "mediaID": "IMG-20240315-142300",
  "deploymentID": "dep_456",
  "fileMediaType": "image/jpeg",
  "filePath": "https://storage.example.com/media/IMG-20240315-142300.jpg",
  "filePublic": false,
  "exifData": {
    "DateTimeOriginal": "2024-03-15T14:23:00",
    "Make": "Bushnell",
    "Model": "Trophy Cam"
  },
  "observations": ["obs_001"]
}
```

</CodeGroup>

## Create Media

Create a new media record.

### Hook

```typescript
useCreateMediaMutation()
```

### Request Parameters

<ParamField path="deploymentID" type="string" optional>
  Associated deployment ID
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="fileMediaType" type="string" optional>
  MIME type of the file
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="filePath" type="string" optional>
  URL or path to the file
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="filePublic" type="boolean" optional>
  Public accessibility flag
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="exifData" type="object" optional>
  EXIF metadata from the file
</ParamField>

### Response

Returns the created `Media` object with auto-generated `id` and `mediaID`.

### Usage Example

<CodeGroup>

```typescript Component
import { useCreateMediaMutation } from '@/redux/api/media'
import * as ImagePicker from 'expo-image-picker'
import * as FileSystem from 'expo-file-system'

function UploadMedia({ deploymentId }) {
  const [createMedia, { isLoading }] = useCreateMediaMutation()

  const handlePickImage = async () => {
    const result = await ImagePicker.launchImageLibraryAsync({
      mediaTypes: ImagePicker.MediaTypeOptions.Images,
      quality: 1,
      exif: true,
    })

    if (result.canceled) return

    try {
      // Upload file to storage first
      const uploadResult = await uploadToStorage(result.assets[0].uri)
      
      // Create media record
      const media = await createMedia({
        deploymentID: deploymentId,
        fileMediaType: 'image/jpeg',
        filePath: uploadResult.url,
        filePublic: false,
        exifData: result.assets[0].exif,
      }).unwrap()
      
      console.log('Media created:', media.mediaID)
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Failed to upload media:', error)
    }
  }

  return (
    <button onClick={handlePickImage} disabled={isLoading}>
      {isLoading ? 'Uploading...' : 'Upload Image'}
    </button>
  )
}
```

```typescript Request
{
  "deploymentID": "dep_456",
  "fileMediaType": "image/jpeg",
  "filePath": "https://storage.example.com/media/IMG-20240315-142300.jpg",
  "filePublic": false,
  "exifData": {
    "DateTimeOriginal": "2024-03-15T14:23:00",
    "Make": "Bushnell",
    "Model": "Trophy Cam"
  }
}
```

```typescript Response
{
  "id": "media_789",
  "mediaID": "IMG-20240315-142300",
  "deploymentID": "dep_456",
  "fileMediaType": "image/jpeg",
  "filePath": "https://storage.example.com/media/IMG-20240315-142300.jpg",
  "filePublic": false,
  "exifData": {
    "DateTimeOriginal": "2024-03-15T14:23:00",
    "Make": "Bushnell",
    "Model": "Trophy Cam"
  }
}
```

</CodeGroup>

## Update Media

Update an existing media record.

### Hook

```typescript
useUpdateMediaMutation()
```

### Request Parameters

The request body must include the `id` field along with other fields to update.

<ParamField path="id" type="string" required>
  Media ID to update
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="..." type="MediaCreate" required>
  Other media fields to update
</ParamField>

### Response

Returns the updated `Media` object.

### Usage Example

<CodeGroup>

```typescript Component
import { useUpdateMediaMutation } from '@/redux/api/media'

function ToggleMediaVisibility({ media }: { media: Media }) {
  const [updateMedia, { isLoading }] = useUpdateMediaMutation()

  const handleToggle = async () => {
    try {
      const updated = await updateMedia({
        id: media.id,
        deploymentID: media.deploymentID,
        fileMediaType: media.fileMediaType,
        filePath: media.filePath,
        filePublic: !media.filePublic,
        exifData: media.exifData,
      }).unwrap()
      
      console.log('Media visibility updated:', updated.filePublic)
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Failed to update media:', error)
    }
  }

  return (
    <button onClick={handleToggle} disabled={isLoading}>
      {isLoading ? 'Updating...' : media.filePublic ? 'Make Private' : 'Make Public'}
    </button>
  )
}
```

```typescript Request
{
  "id": "media_789",
  "deploymentID": "dep_456",
  "fileMediaType": "image/jpeg",
  "filePath": "https://storage.example.com/media/IMG-20240315-142300.jpg",
  "filePublic": true
}
```

</CodeGroup>

## Delete Media

Delete a media file by ID.

### Hook

```typescript
useDeleteMediaMutation()
```

### Parameters

<ParamField path="id" type="string" required>
  Media ID to delete
</ParamField>

### Response

Returns `void` on success.

### Usage Example

<CodeGroup>

```typescript Component
import { useDeleteMediaMutation } from '@/redux/api/media'

function DeleteMedia({ mediaId }: { mediaId: string }) {
  const [deleteMedia, { isLoading }] = useDeleteMediaMutation()

  const handleDelete = async () => {
    if (!confirm('Delete this media file?')) return
    
    try {
      await deleteMedia(mediaId).unwrap()
      console.log('Media deleted')
      // Optionally delete from storage as well
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Failed to delete media:', error)
    }
  }

  return (
    <button onClick={handleDelete} disabled={isLoading}>
      {isLoading ? 'Deleting...' : 'Delete'}
    </button>
  )
}
```

</CodeGroup>

## Working with EXIF Data

### Extracting Location from EXIF

Extract GPS coordinates from image metadata:

```typescript
const { data: media } = useGetMediaByIdQuery(mediaId)

const getLocationFromExif = (exifData: Record<string, any>) => {
  if (!exifData) return null
  
  const lat = parseFloat(exifData.GPSLatitude)
  const lng = parseFloat(exifData.GPSLongitude)
  
  if (isNaN(lat) || isNaN(lng)) return null
  
  return { latitude: lat, longitude: lng }
}

const location = media?.exifData ? getLocationFromExif(media.exifData) : null
```

### Camera Information

Display camera details from EXIF:

```typescript
const getCameraInfo = (exifData: Record<string, any>) => {
  if (!exifData) return null
  
  return {
    make: exifData.Make,
    model: exifData.Model,
    dateTime: exifData.DateTimeOriginal,
    flash: exifData.Flash,
    focalLength: exifData.FocalLength,
  }
}
```

## Filtering Media

### By Deployment

```typescript
const { data: allMedia } = useGetMediaQuery()

const getMediaByDeployment = (deploymentId: string) => {
  return allMedia?.filter(m => m.deploymentID === deploymentId)
}
```

### By Media Type

```typescript
const { data: allMedia } = useGetMediaQuery()

const images = allMedia?.filter(m => m.fileMediaType?.startsWith('image/'))
const videos = allMedia?.filter(m => m.fileMediaType?.startsWith('video/'))
```

### Unclassified Media

```typescript
const { data: allMedia } = useGetMediaQuery()

const unclassified = allMedia?.filter(
  m => !m.observations || m.observations.length === 0
)
```
